column1: DEXTER / MIDAS — Jira Import: Full Program Backlog (v8, 2026-03-31)

column7: Summary
column12: Description (truncated)

column7: Fix cron UTC offset: 45 5 → 10 45 (fires at 01:45 ET, not 05:45 ET)
column12: Current: 45 5 * * * UTC = 01:45 ET. Target: 10 45 * * 1-5 UTC = 05:45 ET EST. This means the ETL runs in the middle of the night on yesterday's data. Fix before next market day.

column7: Remove API keys from cron prompt body — move to env vars
column12: FINNHUB_KEY, FRED_API_KEY, SLACK_WEBHOOK are in plaintext in the cron prompt string. Anyone with cron config access sees all three credentials. Move to Azure VM environment variables or Key Vault. ETL

column7: Fix SLACK_WEBHOOK double-fire — remove from cron prompt body
column12: SLACK_WEBHOOK in both prompt body and delivery config. ETL already posts its own Slack alert at run end. Remove from prompt body to prevent duplicate messages on every run.

column7: Add dexter_notebooklm_upload.py as cron step 3 (after ETL, before Phase 0)
column12: HEARTBEAT 05:55 step. Must run after ETL (which creates the files) and before Phase 0 (which reads them from NotebookLM). All source files must be in notebook before OpenClaw synthesises.

column7: Add OpenClaw Phase 0-5 as cron step 6
column12: Signal Aggregator through GMISR Reporter entirely absent from cron. Phase 0→0.5(conditional)→1→2→3→4→5 must be scheduled after NotebookLM upload completes.

column7: Add DAILY_STATE.md update as cron step 7 (after Phase 5)
column12: Phase 5 output feeds DAILY_STATE.md carry-over. Currently not scheduled. Shadow journal section must be filled manually before re-opening NotebookLM.

column7: Rename feedmd_config_03-01-2026.yml to date-agnostic name
column12: Hardcoded date in config filename. Will silently use a March 1st config indefinitely. Rename to feedmd_config.yml or derive name dynamically.

column7: Restrict cron to weekdays (1-5) and confirm weekend intent
column12: Currently * * * = 7 days/week. Weekend ETL produces stale data. Recommend 1-5 unless Saturday/Sunday runs are intentional. Weekend data could confuse Monday Phase 1 regime read.

column7: Create staging/ directory structure and write_staged() helper

column7: Implement pre-write input audit (Audit step 1)
column12: audit_input(module, data, rules) → (passed, errors). Per module: FRED all 9 series non-None; market VIX/DGS10/one equity; EDGAR P1 or P2 hit; news 3+ feeds. Fail: write DEGRADED to staging, append dea

column7: Implement post-write output audit (Audit step 2)

column7: Implement three audit failure strategies (Fail/Dispatch/Ignore)
column12: Per-module AUDIT_FAILURE_STRATEGY: Fail (FRED), Dispatch to dead-letter (Treasury/market), Ignore+DEGRADED (news). TIER_AUDIT reflects which strategy fired.

column7: Define expected schemas for all external API responses

column7: Implement validate_schema() and inject into fetch steps
column12: validate_schema(data, schema) → (compatible, drift_report). Three outcomes: compatible; forward-compatible (new optionals, log); backward-incompatible (required missing, → SCHEMA_DRIFT dead_letter). I

column7: Write SCHEMA_DRIFT report and auto-update optional field registry
column12: SCHEMA_DRIFT_{date}.txt when new optionals appear. Auto-learn: new field 3 runs in a row → add to optional list with comment. Backward-incompatible → CRITICAL flag, halt module.

column7: Define idempotency granularity and document the contract
column12: run_date (YYYYMMDD) is the idempotency partition key. dexter_data/YYYYMMDD/ is one atomic unit. Two runs for same date produce identical output (same API responses).

column7: Fix FRED_SUMMARY append bug — most urgent idempotency violation

column7: Implement atomic write via stage-then-rename (write_atomic)
column12: write_atomic(path, content): write to path.with_suffix('.tmp'), then os.replace(tmp→final). Atomic on POSIX. Add startup cleanup of *.tmp orphans. Replaces write_file() everywhere.

column7: Add run MANIFEST.json with schema version and ETL script hash

column7: Make EDGAR incremental with delta-column state file (edgar_state.json)
column12: State file with last_successful_fetch_dt. since = max(last_fetch, today-14d). After successful write update state. 14-day backfill window. Eliminates ghost-filing problem.

column7: --force-rerun flag and idempotency test procedure
column12: CLI flag: delete manifest, clear staging, re-run all. Test: run twice for same date, diff output — byte-identical except TIER_AUDIT timestamp.

column7: Implement HDS auto-scoring in run_news()
column12: Compute Headline Density Score per tier: count of matching headlines per tier_keywords group / total items. Write HDS per tier to NEWS_CORPUS header section. Top 3 themes by count = blob detection.

column7: Implement VPI (Volatility Pricing Index) in build_tier_audit()
column12: VPI = VIX_spot / (5-day realized vol proxy). VIX > realized → VIX lagging = VPI > 1 (underpriced stress). Requires 5-day VIX history from RUN_HISTORY.log. Add to TIER_AUDIT.

column7: Implement LFI (Liquidity Friction Index) in build_tier_audit()
column12: LFI = composite of: FRA-OIS normalised (0-1 over 0-60bps range) + BDC price discount to NAV proxy + EDGAR 8-K withdrawal/redemption count. Write to TIER_AUDIT.

column7: Implement NLLI (Narrative Lag-Lead Indicator) in run_news()
column12: Compare current run's tier headline counts to prior 5-run rolling average. Rising count in tier = positive NLLI for that tier. Write NLLI vector to NEWS_CORPUS header.

column7: Implement CCS (Cross-Correlation Score) in run_market()
column12: CCS = correlation between ratio-pair movements (TQQQ/SQQQ, Gold/NASDAQ etc) vs broad index. Low CCS = idiosyncratic alpha opportunity. Requires 5-day price history.

column7: Implement CXOD (Cross-Asset Order Delta) in run_market()
column12: CXOD = signed flow divergence: (USO 1d return) - (QQQ 1d return). Positive = energy bid, equities sold. Negative = risk-on. Write to COMMODITIES/EQUITIES files.

column7: Implement PSI (Policy Sensitivity Index) in build_tier_audit()
column12: PSI = composite of: GDP revision magnitude + FRA-OIS level + high-impact events within 5 days + earnings guidance revision count. Higher PSI = higher policy error risk.
